cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why a large number of people come to Pompeii each year based on the provided passage. Let me read through the text again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage starts by saying that every year thousands of tourists visit Pompeii to see the sights it's famous for, like the stadium, theaters, shops, and restaurants. But it also mentions that no one has lived there for almost 2000 years. The rest of the text explains that Pompeii was destroyed by Mount Vesuvius in 79 AD and was buried under ashes and stones. In 1861, Giuseppe started uncovering the city, and now scientists have found many artifacts and structures that tell us about how the people of Pompeii lived. The last paragraph mentions that scientists are still making discoveries that attract tourists. The question is asking for the main reason people come to Pompeii each year. The options are: (A) To visit the volcano. (B) To shop and eat there. (C) To watch sports and plays. (D) To see how Pompeiians lived. Looking at the passage, the first paragraph mentions that tourists see things like the stadium and theaters, shops and restaurants. But since no one actually lives there, the actual purpose is not for current shopping or dining. The key point is that the city was preserved and uncovered, revealing daily life. The third paragraph explains that the most important finds are everyday objects that tell us a lot about the people, like their preference for wine, bread, and makeup. The last paragraph says that the discoveries made by Giuseppe and the ongoing work by scientists are what draw tourists. So, even though the first paragraph lists the stadium, theaters, shops, and restaurants as the sights, the real reason is that those are the remnants of the ancient city that show how the Pompeiians lived. The actual experience is historical, not for current activities. The other options don't fit. Visiting the volcano (A) isn't the main point since Vesuvius is nearby but Pompeii itself is the attraction. Shopping and eating (B) are mentioned
